item.title: Munchausen is trending 💁🏻‍♀️Please note: I learned a...
item.description:
View on Instagram


Munchausen is trending 💁🏻‍♀️Please note: I learned a lesson; some topics are off limits in the realm of satirical comedy. I do want to reiterate that my intention behind this post was to call out people who flippantly claim they have hundreds of issues they clearly know nothing about. People like that make it harder for those with real concerns to gain support, understanding, and help. I thought that was clear, but I see that it wasn’t. As an alcoholic in recovery with a diagnosed phobia and a family member with severe Crohn’s, I would NEVER make fun of people with chronic illness or disabilities of any kind. I apologize to those I have upset. #asheville #mentalhealth #friendship #empath

item.title: It’s #828 vs #864. AVL vs. GVLIt’s been six weeks since Hurr...
item.description:
View on Instagram


It’s #828 vs #864. AVL vs. GVLIt’s been six weeks since Hurricane Helene swept through Asheville and the surrounding areas, leaving communities reeling and rebuilding from profound loss. While much of the media has moved on, the devastation here is still real, and the recovery has only just begun. Local businesses and families are struggling to keep going, and they need our help now more than ever.In times like these, humor has always been a way to cope—a tool to process pain and find light, even in the darkest places. But as we move through this shared trauma, what feels appropriate is complicated. Laughter doesn’t erase the devastation; instead, it reminds us of our resilience and our shared humanity. It brings us together, if only for a moment, in a time when so much feels broken. These moments of connection, even through laughter, remind us that we’re not alone—that healing is possible.Please don’t let Asheville and the surrounding areas slip from your mind. Our communities still need your support. Every donation, every moment of care, and even every shared moment of laughter keeps hope alive here. Together, we can rebuild, step by step. - CC & JT #avl #gvl #nc #sc #comedy

item.title: @valhalla.axe.asheville is one of many amazing local businesses ...
item.description:
View on Instagram


@valhalla.axe.asheville is one of many amazing local businesses that could use some sweet, sweet lovin’! In all seriousness, we had so much fun, and no Ryans were harmed in the making of this video. Now is a great time to branch out and try some new things in support of WNC locals/small business owners ♥️#datenight #date #asheville #supportlocal #supportsmallbusiness

item.title: Five weeks have passed. Under normal circumstances, the city wou...
item.description:
View on Instagram


Five weeks have passed. Under normal circumstances, the city would be alive with the vibrant energy of visitors, here to revel in the area’s natural beauty. But now, the streets are empty and silent, with piles of debris as solemn reminders of what was. The local businesses still standing are fighting to survive, doors open but futures uncertain. Our community needs people—not just to visit, but to invest in its resilience, to help rebuild what’s been lost.There’s a deep sense of mourning here, not only for what was physically taken but for the dreams these spaces held. I remember walking into DayTrip before it opened, seeing the space that Brandon and Davie had so lovingly curated. They showed us their thrifted treasures—a whimsical owl lamp from the ’70s, a psychedelic tapestry, tables layered with vintage recipes and vibrant colors that made each piece feel alive. Now, all of it is gone. Swept away by the storm. The mud-streaked piles of broken glassware, once-dazzling disco balls—each a fragment of years of work and dedication, gone in an instant.This is just one story among many. So many individuals, families, and business owners are left adrift, struggling to envision their next steps. Communities affected by the storm need continued support to help these dreams find footing again. This is only the beginning, and we can’t rebuild alone. #asheville #ashevillenc #smallbusiness #supportlocal #shoplocal #shopsmall

item.title: Blind Date Live is thrilled to announce our next show on Februar...
item.description:
View on Instagram


Blind Date Live is thrilled to announce our next show on February 7 will be at the Orange Peel! We’re so grateful for the support of Story Parlor and the Grey Eagle—their spaces have truly shaped and lifted this show, and we couldn’t love them more. But with each performance, we’re growing, and we’re excited to bring the absurdity to a bigger stage!We know things are more than a little strange right now, and times have been tough after Helene, but we want to bring a little extra joy and laughter to Asheville. If you’d like to join us but can’t swing a ticket, just DM us—we want this night to be accessible to everyone who needs a break and a good laugh. We look forward to seeing you in February ♥️ And if you happen to be in Charlotte on November 22, please come support! We love you so much. So, so much. P.S. This will be our first show without our long-time co-producers @doubledippro but we love them very much, please support them, too! #asheville #ashevillenc #blinddate #comedy
